title: HTTP/1.1 Response Serialization, Field Order, Framing, and Writes
description: A writer emits content before headers, injects unchecked field values, chooses conflicting framing, assumes one Write completes, and cannot prove exact transmitted bytes. Learners cumulatively produce canonical bounded HTTP/1.1 response serializer through theory, guided work, debugging, transfer, retrieval, and assessment.
objectives: Commit one status line, validated field section, empty line, framed content, and trailers in protocol order.
Reject CR, LF, NUL, controls, invalid names, and unsafe values before serializing any response field.
Choose no-content, fixed-length, chunked, or close-delimited output from method, status, known length, streaming, version, and reuse policy.
